meaning: n.斯拉夫人[语]adj.斯拉夫民族的
examples: The Ukrainians speak a Slavonic language similar to Russian.
Slavonic Dance No. 11 for piano, 4 hands in F major.
The Ukrainians speak a Slavonic language similar to Russian .
Slavonic and Albanian peoples have lived side by side in Kosovo since the eighth century.
Dvorak's Slavonic Dances are familiar as individual numbers, often played as encores or musical amuse-bouches before the main event of a concert.
